title: Respect kernel/user space separation
description: Keep policy and large/variable work in user space and only the privileged mechanism in the kernel, exposing a small, stable, validated interface.
body: Use when deciding what belongs in the kernel. Do not push business logic, parsing of complex formats, or policy into the kernel where a bug is a panic or exploit, and do not break the stable user ABI. Actions: keep the kernel to the minimal privileged mechanism, expose a narrow validated syscall/ioctl/sysfs interface, and leave policy to user space. Validation signals include a small reviewed interface surface and ABI-compatibility tests passing for existing user programs. Risk boundary: every line added to the kernel runs with full privilege and widens the attack surface; escalate any proposal to move substantial logic into the kernel for a security and ABI review.
